sglang.title: [4/N] (Elastic EP) Back up Expert Weights in DRAM
sglang.author: ympcMark
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Currently, in Elastic EP, when rank failures or expert weight loss occur, the system must reload missing weights from disk. This process significantly increases service interruption time.
We observed that servers often have unused spare DRAM capacity. Leveraging this available DRAM, we pr

vllm.title: [ROCm] Add extra step in config initialization to populate custom ops before compilation config init
vllm.author: gshtras
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Prerequisite for #33271
When AITER is enabled, certain custom ops are appended to the compilation config on ROCm based on the AITER ops.
Currently this is done after the
default_config = OPTIMIZATION_LEVEL_TO_CONFIG[self.optimization_level]

initialization, so the related fusion passes can't happen.

vllm.title: [Bugfix][Hardware][AMD] Gate FP4 ops on gfx950 to prevent MI300X crash
vllm.author: c0de128
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
FP4 BMM and FP4 ASM GEMM use CDNA4-only instructions available only on gfx950. Without a hardware check, enabling VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_FP4BMM or VLLM_ROCM_USE_AITER_FP4_ASM_GEMM on MI300X (gfx942) would crash with illegal instruction errors.

Add on_gfx950() gate to is_fp4bmm_enabled() and is

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix uint32 overflow in Mamba selective scan state pointer arithmetic
vllm.author: Josephasafg
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Fixed SSM state corruption for Mamba models under high concurrency.
SSMParamsBase::index_t was uint32_t, causing cache_index * ssm_states_batch_stride to overflow for large cache indices.
This made the kernel write SSM state to wrong memory addresses, leaving the intended slots zeroed. Subse

vllm.title: [ROCm] Add dynamic mxfp4 quantization for DeepSeek V2 projection layers
vllm.author: dllehr-amd
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Enables runtime dynamic quantization for projection layers in DeepSeek V2 when using Quark OCP MX quantization. This approach allows excluded layers (except lm_head) to be quantized dynamically at model load time rather than using pre-quantized weights.
Changes:

Add dynamic_mxfp4_quant flag to Quar

After this change I'm able to build vLLM on an MI300X machine running ROCm 7.1 with the following commands.
When choosing a new amdsmi version, I just picked the most recent release on pypy (https://pypi.org/project/amdsmi/#history)
uv pip install -r requirements/rocm-build.txt --index-strat

vllm.title: [BugFix][kv_offload]: Fix kernel block size detection
vllm.author: orozery
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The kernel block size may be incorrectly detected where we register a non-cross layers kv-cache.
In particular, this happens when using the HND layout.

vllm.title: Add GlmOcrConfig for GLM-OCR model type recognition
vllm.author: hujia177
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary:
The GLM-OCR model (zai-org/GLM-OCR) uses model_type: "glm_ocr" in its config.json, which is not recognized by the bundled HuggingFace Transformers library (requires transformers >= 5.1.0). This causes a ValidationError during ModelConfig creation when vLLM tries to load the model via AutoCo

vllm.title: [Perf] Optimize maxsim scores computation for pooling models, 13.9% E2E throughput improvement
vllm.author: yewentao256
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Optimize maxsim scores computation for pooling models
Originally it is calculated in CPU, now we calculate it in GPU and using the batched version, so we get a lot of performance improvement
Test
Acc
Covered in unit tests
tests/entrypoints/pooling/score/test_online_colbert.py::TestColBERTOnl

vllm.title: [Core] Support `min_tokens` with speculative decoding
vllm.author: qianlihuang
vllm.bodyExcerpt:

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Remove erroneous lower bound on LoRA vocab size constraint
vllm.author: LucasWilkinson
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FIX #35353
Summary
Fixes ValueError: When using LoRA, vocab size must be > 32000 and <= 258048 that breaks Mixtral LoRA tests.
Root Cause
PR #34773 intended to increase the LoRA vocab size upper bound from 257024 to 258048, but accidentally introduced a lower bound check.
The original condition was:

vllm.title: [Model Runner V2] Fix error-handling
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
If execute_model() raises an exception, the core logic expects the subsequent sample_tokens() call to return None (with async scheduling / PP we don't wait for the result from execute_model before calling sample_tokens).
Without this change, we fail with a different exception and the root cause exce

vllm.title: fix(reasoning): Qwen3ReasoningParser returns truncated output as reasoning
vllm.author: stakeswky
vllm.bodyExcerpt:

When the model output is truncated (no </think> token), Qwen3ReasoningParser.extract_reasoning() incorrectly returned (None, model_output), treating all reasoning content as regular content. Since Qwen3 defaults to thinking enabled, a missing </think> means the output was cut off mid-thought

vllm.title: [Bugfix] Fix MessageQueue connect_ip for cross-node data parallelism
vllm.author: luccafong
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

When using multi-node data parallelism (DP > 1, nnodes > 1), each DP group leader's MessageQueue was binding its ZMQ socket to parallel_config.master_addr (the head node's IP). For DP groups whose leader resides on a non-master node, this caused zmq.error.ZMQError: Cannot assign requested a

vllm.title: [WideEP] Remove pplx all2all backend
vllm.author: tlrmchlsmth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR removes the PPLX all2all kernels.
The pplx-kernels all2all backend uses https://github.com/perplexityai/pplx-kernels, but this has been replaced by https://github.com/perplexityai/pplx-garden.
pplx-garden does not have the same interface as pplx-kernels so isn't an easy drop-in replacement,

vllm.title: [Kernel][perf] optimize NCCL symm_mem vs custom_AR selection thresholds
vllm.author: pkousha
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Optimizes the decision logic for when to use NCCL symmetric memory allreduce vs custom allreduce based on H100 and GB200 benchmarks.
Problem
The previous threshold-based approach only used NCCL symm_mem for large tensors (≥2MB for 4 GPUs, ≥1MB for 8 GPUs). However, benchmarks show that symm_

When using tensor parallelism (TP) combined with pipeline parallelism (PP), flashinfer's trtllm_create_ipc_workspace_for_all_reduce_fusion function uses tp_rank as the CUDA device index (torch.device("cuda", tp_rank).index). However, in PP scenarios, tp_rank is relative to the TP group withi

vllm.title: [Bug] correct out dtype of rms_norm_gated native path
vllm.author: zufangzhu
vllm.bodyExcerpt: RMSNorm-style ops should return an output tensor with the same dtype as x (the activation dtype). Internal upcasting for stability is fine, but the result should be cast back before returning.  In RMSNormGated.forward_native, the computation can end up in FP32 because self.eps defaults to the Python

Purpose
For Row Parallel Linear layers, the partial results from different TP ranks should be summed, not averaged. This PR changes the reduce_op to "sum" to ensure the output magnitude is mathematically correct.
